op.text: Completed 2025-07-12: First sail of the Dorothy Jean occurred as planned on the Columbia River; Sam, Marcus, and Nina (four months old) attended; gentle afternoon sail; photo taken of Nina asleep in tiny life jacket against Marcus's chest with mainsail up; sky was high thin clouds (close to mare's tail); Dana considered it a blessing

2025-07-12: The Dorothy Jean sailed for the first time. Gentle afternoon on the Columbia, Sam working the lines, Marcus flew out with Nina. There's a photo of Nina asleep in a tiny life jacket against Marcus's chest with the mainsail up behind them, and Dana said 'I have already had it printed twice.' The sky was high thin clouds, not quite a mare's tail but close, and Dana decided that counted as a blessing. (2025-07-19)

[as of 2025-07-19] Shipped interoperability platform at work (the project fought for since February roadmap review, eighteen months of politics); went live successfully; CEO gave shoutout by name in all-hands

2025-07-19: On her identity and work: 'here's the thing about me you should file away: for fifteen years I have basically been the person who is her work. Not in a grim way — I mean it like an engine. The next launch, the next rung, the harder problem. That's how I've always known I'm okay. It's how I know who I am. I'm the one who never turns down the bigger role. Ambition's been my whole spine.' (2025-07-19)

2025-07-19: On her identity and work: 'here's the thing about me you should file away: for fifteen years I have basically been the person who is her work. Not in a grim way — I mean it like an engine. The next launch, the next rung, the harder problem. That's how I've always known I'm okay. It's how I know who I am. outcomes.detail: minted identity belief bel-45 from sal-44

op.text: 2025-07-19: On shipping the interoperability platform (biggest professional moment of her life): 'I stood there in the launch channel with all the confetti emojis and felt... nothing. Not modest-nothing. Actual, echoing nothing.' and 'I hit the biggest professional moment of my life and it was like biting into wax fruit.' Attributed it to grief: 'Everything's been a half-step muted since Mom in a way that still ambushes me.' (2025-07-19)
